campaign_name: El hilo del horizonte / The Thread of the Horizon

posts.copy_es:
El horizonte existe una sola vez en cada momento. Si lo miras dos veces, no es el mismo.

Hay un hilo — a veces lo notas, a veces no. Donde el cielo y el mar se vuelven el mismo azul y la diferencia deja de importar. No es una vista. Es una condición. Habitar este sitio es vivir dentro de esa condición, no frente a ella.

Casa Horizonte. Cerritos, BCS. En proceso.
Serie Habitar.
posts.copy_en:
The blue of distance is not one blue. It shifts with the hour, the cloud cover, the second time you look.

There is a thread — present always, visible sometimes — where sky and sea dissolve into the same color. What the house is being built to do is place you inside that dissolving, not in front of it. This is the difference between a view and a condition. It is the whole project.

Casa Horizonte. Cerritos, BCS. In process.
Habitar series.
posts.alt_copy_es:
Un hilo que a veces se nota fácilmente y otras no tanto. El horizonte como condición de habitar — siempre presente, nunca el mismo.

Este es el argumento del proyecto. No la vista. La condición.

Casa Horizonte. En proceso.
posts.notes: This is the anchor post — the single most load-bearing piece of copy in the campaign. The Spanish opens with the architects' own register, drawing directly from the sensory thesis without reproducing it wholesale. The English opens from the emotional interior — 'the blue of distance' becomes the studio's own phrase here, not a Solnit citation. Do not publish this post as a draft or placeholder. If the image is not final, hold the post. The alt copy is briefer and more declarative — use it only if Ileana selects a single very strong image where the thesis text would compete with the frame. For a paired carousel (rain first, sun second), use the primary copy_es and copy_en — the longer form earns the paired set.

posts.copy_es:
Enero, Cerritos. El viento tiene sal. La vegetación raspa. La arena es blanca y el mar no tiene color porque el cielo tampoco lo tiene.

Antes de trazar una línea, el sitio te pide que te quedes quieto. Que mires. Que cuentes cuántas veces el horizonte desaparece en la nube y cuántas regresa.

Así empieza el trabajo.
posts.copy_en:
January, Cerritos. The wind carries salt. The scrub catches at your jacket. The sand is bleached and the sea has no color because the sky above it doesn't either.

Before anything is drawn, the site asks you to be still. To look. To count how many times the horizon disappears into cloud and comes back.

That is where the work begins.
posts.notes: Field-note register throughout. Second-person singular implicit — the reader is placed at the site, not told about it. The images should be from the January–February 2023 rain documentation: grey, unpolished, site as it is. If Ileana includes a figure in any frame, it should be incidental to scale — not staged for mood. Do not add any caption language about the architects 'visiting' or 'studying' the site — that explanatory framing collapses the field-note register immediately.

posts.youtube_description:
Cerritos, enero. La lluvia sobre el Pacífico cambia el horizonte — no lo borra, lo revela de otra manera. El gris no es la ausencia del azul. Es una versión más honesta de lo mismo.

Este corto usa el registro documental de la visita al sitio: viento, surf, el borde donde la vegetación termina y la arena comienza. No hay música porque el sitio tiene su propio sonido. No hay narración porque el horizonte no necesita explicación.

Casa Horizonte es un proyecto en proceso en Cerritos, BCS. La tesis del proyecto es que el horizonte no es una vista — es una condición. Una condición que cambia con el clima, con la hora, con cuántas veces la hayas mirado. La casa se diseña para colocarte dentro de esa condición, no frente a ella.

Este es el primero de los registros de proceso. Hay más por venir.

—

Cerritos, January. Rain over the Pacific changes the horizon — it doesn't erase it, it reveals it differently. The grey is not the absence of blue. It is a more honest version of the same thing.

This short film uses the site documentation footage: wind, surf, the edge where scrub ends and sand begins. There is no music because the site has its own sound. There is no narration because the horizon does not require explanation.

Casa Horizonte is an in-process project in Cerritos, BCS. Its thesis is that the horizon is not a view — it is a condition. One that changes with the weather, the hour, the number of times you have looked. The house is designed to place you inside that condition, not in front of it.

This is the first of the process records. More to come.

Arquitectura: Oficio Taller — Andrea San Román, Agustina Maza, Diego Tamayo, Rocio Sepúlveda, Nuria Ramirez, Gerardo Ramirez, Gabriela Salinas, Marcela González
Interiorismo: Mariana de la Garza e Iván Zúñiga
Paisaje: Brenda Landeros
Ubicación: Cerritos, Baja California Sur, México
Año: 2023–presente, en construcción
Serie: Habitar

#oficiotaller #casahorizonte #habitarseries #arquitecturamexicana #bajacaliforniasur #mexicanarchitecture #architecturefilm #processdocumentation #coastalarchitecture #architectureandnature #arquitecturaresidencial #elpacífico
posts.notes: YouTube title should follow the studio's naming pattern: 'Oficio Taller | Casa Horizonte | El horizonte en gris'. The youtube_description field above contains the full bilingual description for the YouTube post. This sequence is contingent on the rain footage being in an uploadable state — if not, hold this slot and move sequence 4 forward. When posted to YouTube, Canal should share a link from Instagram Stories or a dedicated grid post (a separate manual action). Sound design note: the ambient site audio — wind, surf — IS the score. Do not add music. Do not add narration. Duration maximum 4 minutes.

posts.copy_es:
Hay lugares que te forman la necesidad. No de estar ahí, sino de seguir mirando.

En este borde — arena, surf, el Pacífico abriéndose — algo se acumula. Una manera de pararte. Una dirección que el cuerpo recuerda aunque el horizonte ya no esté frente a ti.

La casa nace de eso. No de la vista. De la necesidad.
posts.copy_en:
Some places teach you a need you didn't know you had. Not to return to them, but to keep looking — outward, as far and wide as the edge allows.

Standing at this site — sand, surf, the Pacific opening past all distance — the body learns something. A direction it will remember long after it has left.

The house is built around that. Not the view. The need.
posts.notes: The Williams register ('I must have developed my need to see the horizon') is woven into the fabric of both captions without attribution or citation. The strategy note specifies: since the anchor post leaned into the distance register (Solnit), this post introduces the bodily, physical need (Williams) as the deepening. Do not attribute Williams or Solnit by name in the caption — the literary references are calibration, not content. The image selected by Ileana should have the horizon present and should feel close to the site condition — the human scale at the edge, not a wide architectural establishing shot. One image, horizon visible, the site as it is.

posts.copy_es:
El horizonte ordena la planta. No como referencia visual — como eje de orientación. La casa se organiza para que el hilo esté siempre legible, en cualquier parte del proyecto, en cualquier hora.

Del territorio a la sección. De la condición a la forma.

Casa Horizonte. En construcción.
posts.copy_en:
The horizon organizes the plan. Not as a view to frame — as an axis the entire project orients to. Every room finds it. Every threshold opens toward it. No part of the house loses the thread.

From territory to section. From the condition to its answer in form.

Casa Horizonte. Under construction.
posts.notes: Carousel sequence must follow the order specified in strategy.json: site photograph first — do not open with the sketch or render. Plan/section sketch second. Render last, and only if it is strong enough to carry the weight of four prior posts — if the render quality is insufficient, omit it entirely. The copy is written to work with or without the render in the carousel. The Habitar series frame is present in 'condición' and 'habitar' without being named directly as a series. Diego and Ileana should review the render quality together before this post is scheduled. If render is omitted, the copy still lands — 'De la condición a la forma' can be read from site photo to sketch alone.

posts.website_text:
Casa Horizonte
Cerritos, Baja California Sur, México
2023–presente, en construcción
Serie Habitar

Cerritos es un sitio que impone una condición antes de que alguien decida construir en él. El Pacífico llega sin obstáculos. El viento viene cargado de sal. La vegetación — matorral seco, resistente, bajo — termina donde la arena comienza, y ahí, en ese umbral entre lo terrestre y lo marino, el horizonte se vuelve el elemento más presente del paisaje. No como fondo. Como protagonista.

El equipo de Oficio Taller documentó el sitio en enero y febrero de 2023, en lluvia y en sol. Esa decisión fue parte del proyecto desde el principio: el gris del Pacífico en invierno no es una versión disminuida del azul del verano. Es una condición distinta y con igual peso. La tesis del proyecto nació de esos dos registros: el horizonte existe una sola vez en cada momento, nunca es el mismo si lo miras dos veces, y habitarlo — no verlo, habitarlo — implica aceptar esa condición como el orden central de la casa.

La planta se organiza a partir del horizonte como eje de orientación. Cada espacio del proyecto mantiene una relación activa con ese hilo — a veces clara, a veces apenas perceptible — que conecta el interior con la línea donde el cielo y el mar se vuelven indistinguibles. No se trata de capturar vistas. Se trata de que la experiencia de habitar la casa sea inseparable de la condición que el sitio impone.

El proyecto trabaja con los materiales y el paisaje de la costa del Pacífico bajacaliforniano: lo que el clima produce, lo que la mano de obra local puede hacer, lo que la topografía pide. El interiorismo está a cargo de Mariana de la Garza e Iván Zúñiga. El diseño de paisaje es de Brenda Landeros.

Casa Horizonte está en construcción.

—

Cerritos is a site that establishes its conditions before any construction begins. The Pacific arrives without interruption. The wind is salt-laden. The dry coastal scrub ends where the sand begins, and at that threshold — between land and sea — the horizon becomes the dominant presence in the landscape. Not as background. As subject.

The Oficio Taller team documented the site in January and February 2023, in rain and in sun. That decision was architectural before it was practical: the grey Pacific of winter is not a lesser version of the summer blue. It is a distinct condition with equal weight. The project's thesis emerged from both: the horizon exists only once in each moment, it is never the same if you look at it twice, and to habitar it — not to view it, to inhabitar it — means accepting that condition as the central organizing principle of the house.

The plan is oriented around the horizon as axis. Every space in the project holds an active relationship with the thread — sometimes clear, sometimes barely legible — that connects interior to the line where sky and sea become indistinguishable. This is not about framing views. It is about making the experience of living in the house inseparable from the condition the site demands.

The project works with the materials and landscape of the Pacific coast of Baja California Sur: what the climate produces, what local labor can build, what the topography requires. Interior design: Mariana de la Garza e Iván Zúñiga. Landscape design: Brenda Landeros.

Casa Horizonte is under construction.

—

Arquitectura / Architecture: Oficio Taller
Andrea San Román, Agustina Maza, Diego Tamayo, Rocio Sepúlveda, Nuria Ramirez, Gerardo Ramirez, Gabriela Salinas, Marcela González

Interiorismo / Interiors: Mariana de la Garza e Iván Zúñiga
Paisaje / Landscape: Brenda Landeros

Ubicación / Location: Cerritos, Baja California Sur, México
Año / Year: 2023–presente / 2023–present
Estado / Status: En construcción / Under construction
Serie / Series: Habitar
posts.notes: The website_text field contains the full bilingual project description for the Cargo CMS page. Website publishes one week after sequence 5 — the full Instagram sequence must be complete before the page goes live. Include the full sensory thesis text (not excerpted) on the Cargo page in addition to this project description — the brief.json sensory_thesis field is the source. If the YouTube film (sequence 3) is live by the time this page publishes, embed or link it from the project page. Do not include project completion date, area measurements, pricing, or any amenity language. The Habitar series tag should be present and linked to the series index if one exists in Cargo.
